Links

There are countless useful and life-saving resources for Digital Text Analysis and Digital Literary Studies out there - though it’s sometimes hard to find them (again). Here are some of my favourites:

Stylometry & Text Analytics

Data Science Dojo Youtube series on Text Analytics with R

R & R-Packages

R Code - Best practices
Writing an R package from scratch
For a more thorough discussions packages and their architecture, have a look at Hadley Wickham’s R packages
Hadley Wickham & Garrett Grolemund’s R for Data Science is an unbelievably valuable resource for reading up on some R basics.
Hadley Wickham Advanced R
For non-programmers Garrett Grolemund’s Hands-On Programming with R
R-bloggers
An Introduction to Statistical Learning
In general, Machine Learning Mastery is a great place when you are looking for some tutorials on machine learning in both R & python - the R Machine Learning Mini-Course is especially helpful for absolute beginners who want a step-by-step guide to their first model.

Basics in Statistics

StatQuest with Josh Starmer

GitHub Pages

This tutorial by Amanda Visconti helped me enormously to created this website. I use the design template Gravity, which is not only easy to use, but also already comes with a nice structure for blogs posts.
